message:
fix(ft-serialize): bound RaptorQ sidecar repair_symbols input

Closes bead frankentorch-6q4m. generate_raptorq_sidecar accepted an
arbitrary usize and forwarded it (after a saturating_add(8) headroom)
to asupersync's SystematicEncoder::emit_repair, which preallocates
Vec::with_capacity(count) and uses u32::try_from(i).expect(...) inside
its loop. So values like usize::MAX / u32::MAX+1 (e.g. CLI
--repair-symbols 4294967296) could trigger an OOM allocation or a
"repair count exceeds u32" panic instead of a controlled error.

Two-tier cap:

  MAX_RAPTORQ_REPAIR_SYMBOLS  = 1024   (public, user-input cap)
  RAPTORQ_REPAIR_COUNT_HARD_CAP = 65 536 (internal, post-padding cap)

The user-input cap is well above realistic usage (the CLI default is
4 and the existing fallback budgets only reach 128). The internal
hard cap covers the pathological case where k_prime padding makes
min_repair_symbols itself enormous (RFC 6330 max k_prime is 56403),
catching the odd input that would compute past the user cap from
internal constants.

generate_raptorq_sidecar now fails closed with
SerializeError::IncompatiblePayload before any encoder allocation
happens. CLI run_raptorq_durability_pipeline mirrors the input cap
for a user-facing diagnostic.

Tests:
  raptorq_sidecar_rejects_repair_symbols_above_max: covers MAX+1,
    u32::MAX+1, and usize::MAX (the worst-case OOM trigger);
    confirms the boundary value MAX itself is not rejected by the
    cap (the test tolerates legitimate non-cap rejections from the
    encoder for tiny payloads).

Closed via manual .beads/issues.jsonl append since `br close` failed
on a corrupted SQLite mirror — the JSONL is the source of truth.

Workspace: 74 ft-serialize tests green (73 + 1 new), clippy clean
on ft-serialize and ft-conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
